data.title: Design-conformance PR gate: fail on net-new off-vocabulary colors

data.body: Preventive half of the design-governance loop (companion to the /design-audit periodic sweep). A conformance script extracts the COLOR vocabulary actually used in src/ and compares it against the sanctioned set = the design tokens (parsed live from index.css @theme so it auto-tracks renames) + a baseline allowlist of currently-grandfathered off-token values. Fails (non-zero) only on NET-NEW off-vocabulary values, with a message: conform to a token, OR bless it (add to the allowlist + document the intent in DESIGN.md). Baseline grandfathers today's drift (house-hex hardcodes, page-identity colors, debug colors) so CI is green now; the burn-down shrinks it. Wire via a GitHub Actions workflow on PRs (repo currently has only Netlify CI). Color first; script structured to extend to typography/spacing later. Context: validated by the vocabulary-extractor proof run this session.

Cold review (independent sub-agent, fresh context) vs plan acceptance: PASS. Verified script correctness (token parse from index.css @theme = rename-safe; hex extraction + normalization; SVG-noise skip; file walk; dedup), runtime behavior (clean tree exit 0; new bg-[#hex] + inline color exit 1 with file:line, no dupes; remove returns 0; --update-baseline deterministic), app gates (typecheck/build pass, tests at 7/141 FRAC-199 baseline), GHA workflow valid. One medium finding: bare-hex scan over .ts/.tsx flagged ANY #hex (git refs, comment hexes, prose) → noisy gate. FIXED inline by requiring quoted hex literals (same-quote backref); re-verified comment+gitref no longer flag, quoted color still does, baseline regenerated, clean tree green. Minor non-blocking: workflow node 20 vs netlify node 22 (harmless for zero-dep script). Move to done.
